title: hierochloe borealis var. biflora
intro: Hierochloe borealis var. biflora, also known as sweet grass, is a type of flowering plant in the grass family that is native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of digestive issues, fever, and sore throats. The plant has been valued for its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and heal various health issues, including skin irritations and respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of sweet grass include teas, infusions, and topical applications, which can be used to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and support overall well-being.
